title: Distributed Systems Theory
learningObjectives: Explain **CAP** and **PACELC** with concrete system behaviors, not slogans
Describe **quorum** reasoning and **leader** election failure modes at a high level
Connect **chaos** experiments to **blast** radius controls and learning goals

sections.title: Why Distributed Systems Theory matters
sections.content:
**Distributed systems** interviews test whether you can **name** trade-offs (**CAP**, **latency vs consistency**) and connect them to **operational** patterns—**idempotency**, **retries**, **versioning**, and **observability**.

Messaging days built intuition; this day sharpens **theory** vocabulary for **staff** loops.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Distributed Systems Theory
sections.content:
### Day 67 — **Distributed** **systems** **theory**

### 1. CAP / PACELC
Partition behavior vs steady-state latency trade-offs.

### 2. Consistency models
**Linearizability**, **serializability**, **eventual**.

### 3. Concurrency control
**Optimistic** vs **pessimistic**; **version** fields.

### 4. Time and ordering
**Vector** clocks, **happens-before**, **quorum** ideas.

### 5. Reliability patterns
**Backpressure**, **bulkheads**, **timeouts**.

### 6. Story
**Optimistic** lock without **UX** retry causes **support** spikes; add **version** conflict flows.

sections.title: Basic — CAP one-liner
sections.output:
CAP: during partition, balance consistency vs availability
PACELC: latency vs consistency even without partition

sections.title: Intermediate — Assignment CAP print
Advanced — Version increment toy
sections.description: Simulate OCC conflict on stale version.

sections.title: Quorum read/write overlap
sections.description: R + W > N allows tunable staleness vs durability.
sections.plantuml:
@startuml
title Day 67 — Quorum intuition
note right: Example: N=3, R=2, W=2
participant Writer
participant Reader
database Replicas
Writer -> Replicas : write quorum
Reader -> Replicas : read quorum
@enduml

sections.title: Exercise — Distributed systems theory — CAP (Day 67 assignment)
sections.problem: Print **exactly**: Consistency vs Availability (Partition tolerance assumed)
hints: Name one **metric** and one **config** you would check on-call for this day’s topic.
State a **failure mode** (e.g. rebalance, ISR shrink, schema break) tied to your answer.

sections.title: Use cases — Distributed Systems Theory
sections.content:
## Use cases — **Distributed systems theory**

### 1) **Multi-region** **active-active** **product** **catalog**
**Context:** **Low** **read** **latency** **globally**; **writes** **from** **many** **regions**.
**Why Day 67:** **CAP** **partition** **behavior** and **conflict** **resolution**.
**Implementation outline:**
1. Classify **data** as **strong** **consistency** vs **eventually** **consistent**.
2. Pick **CRDTs**, **last-write-wins**, or **operator** **merge** per **field** **type**.
3. **Exercise** **partition** **drills** between **regions**.
**Failure hook:** **Split-brain** **inventory** **counts** during **network** **cut**.

### 2) **Wallet** **balance** **service**
**Context:** **Financial** **correctness** **trumps** **raw** **availability**.
**Why Day 67:** **Linearizability** **expectations** for **single** **account** **ledger**.
**Implementation outline:**
1. **Single** **writer** **per** **account** **partition** / **shard**.
2. **Quorum** **or** **consensus** **where** **shared** **state** **cannot** **shard**.
3. **Reconciliation** **jobs** for **external** **PSP** **truth**.
**Failure hook:** **Stale** **reads** **expose** **phantom** **balance** after **failover**.

### 3) **Search** **index** **eventually** **consistent** **with** **OLTP**
**Context:** **Postgres** **source** **of** **truth**; **Elasticsearch** **for** **query**.
**Why Day 67:** **Eventual** **consistency** **windows** **visible** **to** **users**.
**Implementation outline:**
1. **Expose** **revision** **tokens** or **timestamps** in **UI** **where** **needed**.
2. **Backfill** **and** **replay** **pipelines** for **repair**.
**Failure hook:** **User** **sees** **deleted** **row** **still** **searchable** **minutes** **later**.

### 4) **Incident** **communication** **under** **partial** **failure**
**Context:** **Checkout** **degraded**; **some** **shards** **healthy**, **others** **not**.
**Why Day 67:** **Blast** **radius** **language** uses **quorum** / **shard** **facts**.
**Implementation outline:**
1. **Status** **page** ties to **error** **budget** and **shard** **map**.
2. **Runbook** **lists** **which** **invariants** **are** **relaxed** **during** **degrade** **mode**.
**Failure hook:** **Over-promising** **“fixed”** when **only** **one** **AZ** **recovered**.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: State **CAP** cleanly.
conceptual.answer: During a **network partition**, you cannot have both **strong linearizable** availability for all operations *and* **full** consistency without compromise—systems pick **CP** or **AP** flavors in practice. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **State **CAP** cleanly.**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: **PACELC** extension?
conceptual.answer: If **partition** (P) then choose **A** vs **C**; **else** (E) choose **latency (L)** vs **consistency (C)** for normal operation. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For ****PACELC** extension?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: Define **linearizability**.
conceptual.answer: Operations appear to occur atomically at **one** point on a **global** timeline matching real-time order observable by concurrent clients. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **Define **linearizability**.**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: Define **serializability**.
conceptual.answer: Outcomes match **some** sequential execution of transactions—as if no interleaving—can differ from linearizability scope. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **Define **serializability**.**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: **Eventual consistency** meaning?
conceptual.answer: If updates stop, replicas **converge** to same state given enough time—does not specify **when**. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For ****Eventual consistency** meaning?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: **Idempotency** key pattern?
conceptual.answer: Client supplies key so **retries** do not duplicate side effects. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: **Optimistic** concurrency control?
conceptual.answer: Detect conflicts on **commit** using **versions**; **retry** on conflict. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?

followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: **Vector clocks** intuition?
conceptual.answer: Track **causal** **precedence** across replicas to detect **concurrent** updates. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail.

followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: **Quorum** **read/write** in replicated DBs?
conceptual.answer: **R + W > N** style rules tune **tolerance** of stale reads vs write durability. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work.

followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: **Exactly-once** illusion?
conceptual.answer: End-to-end **EOS** generally built from **at-least-once** transport + **idempotent** effects + **dedupe**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims.

followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: **BYZANTINE** vs crash faults?
conceptual.answer: **Byzantine** includes **arbitrary** malicious behavior; most cloud systems assume **crash** fault models. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?

conceptual.question: What is **backpressure**?
conceptual.answer: Signaling **upstream** to **slow** down when **downstream** saturated. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?

conceptual.question: **Two generals** problem relevance?
conceptual.answer: Proves **no** deterministic protocol guarantees over **unreliable** links—motivates **timeouts** and **retry** with uncertainty. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations.

conceptual.question: **SLA** vs **SLO** vs **SLI**?
conceptual.answer: **SLI** measured signal, **SLO** internal target, **SLA** contractual promise to customers. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. conceptual.answer: Learn **failure** behavior **proactively** with controlled **experiments**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations.

conceptual.question: When would you choose **RabbitMQ**-style queues over **Kafka** logs?
conceptual.answer: When you need **classic** **work queues**, **per-message** ack competition, and **low-latency** **push** semantics without **replay** as a first-class requirement. **Kafka** wins for **durable**, **replayable**, **high-throughput** **event** backbones.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **When would you choose **RabbitMQ**-style queues over **Kafka** logs?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

conceptual.question: What does the **CAP** trade-off imply for **Kafka** under a partition outage?
conceptual.answer: Per partition, **availability** vs **consistency** shows up as **accepting writes** vs **blocking** on **ISR** (**acks=all** + **min ISR**). Interviewers want **partition-scoped** reasoning, not buzzwords without **operational** levers.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **What does the **CAP** trade-off imply for **Kafka** under a partition outage?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

codeBased.question: Optimistic update SQL.
codeBased.answer: // UPDATE t SET v=?, ver=ver+1 WHERE id=? AND ver=?

followUps.question: What failure mode shows up first when this is misconfigured under load?
followUps.answer: Typical symptoms: **rebalance storms**, **duplicate delivery** after retries, **metadata refresh loops** (**NOT_LEADER**), or **producer buffer exhaustion**. Tie **// UPDATE t SET v=?, ver=ver+1 WHERE id=? AND ver=?** to observable signals before jumping to code changes.

followUps.question: How would you prove correctness in staging without flaky integration tests?
followUps.answer: Use **deterministic** workloads: fixed keys, controlled partition counts, inject broker pause/leader movement in a test cluster, and assert **offset** progress + **exactly-once** expectations with idempotent sinks or test harnesses.

followUps.answer: Use **deterministic** workloads: fixed keys, controlled partition counts, inject broker pause/leader movement in a test cluster, and assert **offset** progress + **exactly-once** expectations with idempotent sinks or test harnesses.

codeBased.question: How does **SNS** fan-out differ from a Kafka **topic** with many consumer groups?
codeBased.answer: **SNS** pushes to **many** **subscribers** (SQS, Lambda, HTTP) operationally distinct from Kafka’s **pull** **consumer groups** reading **one** log. **Cost**, **retry**, and **ordering** models differ—do not conflate them.

followUps.question: What failure mode shows up first when this is misconfigured under load?
followUps.answer: Typical symptoms: **rebalance storms**, **duplicate delivery** after retries, **metadata refresh loops** (**NOT_LEADER**), or **producer buffer exhaustion**. Tie ****SNS** pushes to **many** **subscribers** (SQS, Lambda, HTTP) operationally distinct from Kafka’s *** to observable signals before jumping to code changes.

codeBased.question: What is a **vector clock** useful for in **distributed** data discussions?
codeBased.answer: Capturing **causal** order across **replicas** to detect **concurrent** updates—useful when explaining **conflict** resolution beside **Kafka**’s **ordering** guarantees.

Tie **Capturing **causal** order across **replicas** to detect **concurrent** updates—useful when explaini** to observable signals before jumping to code changes.

seniorScenario.question: **Incident:** **Split** brain after **partition** heals.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Stop writes; snapshot divergent states.

**(2) Root cause** **Automatic** merge impossible safely for money.

**(3) Fix** Manual reconciliation + CRDT only if domain allows.

**(4) Prevention** Playbook. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**.

followUps.question: How do you communicate blast radius and ETA to stakeholders during the incident?
followUps.answer: For ****Incident:** **Split** brain after **partition** heals.**, report **which topics/partitions** lag, **whether writes are still accepted**, **data-loss risk** (ISR), and **consumer backlog time**. Give a **bounded** ETA tied to a concrete mitigation (scale, pause traffic, fail over).

followUps.question: What permanent guardrails prevent a repeat in six months?
followUps.answer: Add **SLOs** on lag and ISR, **game days** for AZ loss and broker restarts, **capacity models** for partitions, and **review gates** for retention/compaction and ACL changes.

seniorScenario.question: **Design:** **Strong** consistency globally for **social** likes.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Challenge product.

**(2) Root cause** Latency + availability hit.

**(3) Fix** AP with **counter** CRDT + UX tolerance.

**(4) Prevention** Metrics. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims.

followUps.question: How do you communicate blast radius and ETA to stakeholders during the incident?
followUps.answer: For ****Design:** **Strong** consistency globally for **social** likes.**, report **which topics/partitions** lag, **whether writes are still accepted**, **data-loss risk** (ISR), and **consumer backlog time**. Give a **bounded** ETA tied to a concrete mitigation (scale, pause traffic, fail over).

followUps.question: What permanent guardrails prevent a repeat in six months?
followUps.answer: Add **SLOs** on lag and ISR, **game days** for AZ loss and broker restarts, **capacity models** for partitions, and **review gates** for retention/compaction and ACL changes.

seniorScenario.question: **Ops:** **Thundering** herd on cache expiry.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Spike to DB.

**(2) Root cause** No jitter.

**(3) Fix** Stale-while-revalidate + jittered TTL.

**(4) Prevention** Load test. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings. seniorScenario.answer:

**(4) Prevention** Diagram. **(4) Prevention** ADR. wrongAnswers: **Eventual** consistency means **immediate** wrong reads ok forever — **Correction:** **Converges** when quiescent.
**Vector** clocks make conflicts disappear — **Correction:** They **detect**; **resolution** still needed.
**Chaos** in prod Friday — **Correction:** **Blast** radius control matters.
**Quorum** math always used in SQL DB — **Correction:** Single-node **primary** common.

sections.title: MCQ Quiz — Distributed Systems Theory
sections.description: Thirty questions from basic to advanced — Distributed Systems Theory. Read every option; distractors are plausible but wrong for a precise reason.

questions.question: [Distributed Systems Theory] Which statement best describes **CAP theorem** in distributed systems?

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.
options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **CAP theorem** belongs to the operational model for **Distributed Systems Theory**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [Distributed Systems Theory] Which option is a **plausible distractor** interviewers use about **linearizability**?

options.A: A precise, bounded statement tied to ordering, ISR, or consumer groups.
options.B: A blanket claim that ignores partition boundaries or client semantics.
options.C: A metric-backed troubleshooting step.
options.D: A configuration paired with a failure mode.

questions.explanation: Watch for absolutes on **linearizability** that ignore **per-partition** semantics and client responsibilities.

questions.question: [Distributed Systems Theory] Incident: user-visible delay worsens after a deploy touching **eventual consistency**. What is a credible first hypothesis?

options.A: The deploy changed timing, batching, retries, or resource limits along the critical path.
options.B: The system randomly deleted all state.
options.C: DNS always causes delay; ignore application logic.
options.D: You always need fewer threads when latency grows.

questions.explanation: Tie **eventual consistency** to measurable regressions: compare golden signals before/after and narrow to the changed surface.

options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **quorum read/write** belongs to the operational model for **Distributed Systems Theory**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [Distributed Systems Theory] You see misconfiguration around **vector clock** in a code review. What is the best first response?

options.A: Map settings to infrastructure policy, then reproduce under load in a controlled environment.
options.B: Delete the topic and recreate it with fewer partitions.
options.C: Set all timeouts to 1ms for safety.
options.D: Disable metrics to reduce noise.

questions.explanation: Validate **vector clock** against defaults, workload, and symptoms (lag, errors, consistency) before changing code.

questions.question: [Distributed Systems Theory] Which statement best describes **vector clock** in distributed systems?

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.
options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **vector clock** belongs to the operational model for **Distributed Systems Theory**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [Distributed Systems Theory] You see misconfiguration around **happens-before** in a code review. What is the best first response?

options.A: Map settings to infrastructure policy, then reproduce under load in a controlled environment.
options.B: Delete the topic and recreate it with fewer partitions.
options.C: Set all timeouts to 1ms for safety.
options.D: Disable metrics to reduce noise.

questions.explanation: Validate **happens-before** against defaults, workload, and symptoms (lag, errors, consistency) before changing code.

questions.question: [Distributed Systems Theory] Incident: user-visible delay worsens after a deploy touching **split brain**. What is a credible first hypothesis?

options.A: The deploy changed timing, batching, retries, or resource limits along the critical path.
options.B: The system randomly deleted all state.
options.C: DNS always causes delay; ignore application logic.
options.D: You always need fewer threads when latency grows.

questions.explanation: Tie **split brain** to measurable regressions: compare golden signals before/after and narrow to the changed surface.

questions.question: [Distributed Systems Theory] Which statement best describes **Byzantine failure** in distributed systems?

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.
options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **Byzantine failure** belongs to the operational model for **Distributed Systems Theory**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [Distributed Systems Theory] You see misconfiguration around **partial failure** in a code review. What is the best first response?

options.A: Map settings to infrastructure policy, then reproduce under load in a controlled environment.
options.B: Delete the topic and recreate it with fewer partitions.
options.C: Set all timeouts to 1ms for safety.
options.D: Disable metrics to reduce noise.

questions.explanation: Validate **partial failure** against defaults, workload, and symptoms (lag, errors, consistency) before changing code.

questions.question: [Distributed Systems Theory] Which option is a **plausible distractor** interviewers use about **idempotency**?

options.A: A precise, bounded statement tied to ordering, ISR, or consumer groups.
options.B: A blanket claim that ignores partition boundaries or client semantics.
options.C: A metric-backed troubleshooting step.
options.D: A configuration paired with a failure mode.

questions.explanation: Watch for absolutes on **idempotency** that ignore **per-partition** semantics and client responsibilities.

sections.title: Distributed Systems Theory — cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Topic | Rule of thumb | Interview one-liner |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Term | Recall | Recall |
| CAP | Partition trade-offs | Partition trade-offs |
| PACELC | Latency vs consistency | Latency vs consistency |
| Linearizable | Real-time global order | Real-time global order |
| Serializable | Transaction interleaving | Transaction interleaving |
| Eventual | Converge when quiet | Converge when quiet |
| OCC | Version conflicts | Version conflicts |
| Vector clock | Causality detection | Causality detection |
| Idempotency key | Safe retries | Safe retries |
| Quorum | R+W>N style | R+W>N style |
| Backpressure | Slow upstream signal | Slow upstream signal |
| Operational metric | Watch lag + ISR + request latency | Say lag per partition and under-replicated partitions |
| Failure drill | Broker bounce + consumer crash chaos | Prove rebalance and recovery SLOs in staging |
| Capacity | Partitions bound parallelism per group | More consumers than partitions stay idle |
| Security | ACLs least privilege per principal | Separate prod cluster or strict topic governance |
